title: Personal Knowledge Wiki Skill
summary: Writer identity produces better knowledge synthesis than filing clerk identity Anti-cramming and anti-thinning balance produces optimal wiki density
creator_view: Writer identity produces better knowledge synthesis than filing clerk identity Anti-cramming and anti-thinning balance produces optimal wiki density

candidates.name: Anti-cramming / anti-thinning balance for knowledge skills
candidates.description:
When to split vs enrich. If adding third paragraph about sub-topic, split. If stub exists with 4 entries mentioning it, enrich. Directly applicable to how /repo-synthesis and /website-synthesis decide granularity of output.

Extraction action: Evaluate EXTRACTIONS.md entries — are any cramming (too many candidates in one source) or thinning (stubs without depth)?

candidates.name: 15-entry checkpoint cycle pattern
candidates.description:
Periodic maintenance rhythm during long operations. Every N items: rebuild indexes, audit quality, check for anti-patterns. Maps to session-end but could be applied mid-session during batch operations.

Extraction action: Compare to convergence loop checkpoints. Could /deep-research benefit from a mid-pipeline quality gate every N findings?

candidates.name: Parallel subagent cleanup workflow (5-batch audit)
candidates.description:
Spawn batches of 5 agents, each audits one article across 6 dimensions (structure, length, tone, quotes, coherence, links). Deduplicate findings, then execute fixes. Directly comparable to our code-reviewer + skill-audit patterns.

Extraction action: Compare agent batch size (5) against our parallelization patterns. Could /skill-audit or /doc-optimizer use this batched audit pattern?

candidates.name: Writer-not-filing-clerk identity framing
candidates.description:
Identity statement shapes every operation: 'what does this mean and how does it connect' vs 'where do I file this'. Applicable to how analysis/synthesis skills should frame their output — synthesis not filing.

Extraction action: Evaluate: do our skills produce synthesis or filing? Does /repo-analysis create understanding or categorization?

candidates.name: Concurrency safety rules for LLM file ops
candidates.description:
Re-read before editing, never delete without reading, rebuild indices only at command end. Practical wisdom already partially captured in CLAUDE.md guardrails but more specific to wiki-style operations.

Extraction action: Compare against CLAUDE.md guardrail #12 (verify file state against filesystem). Any gaps?

candidates.name: 7-command skill architecture as T24 reference design
candidates.description:
The ingest/absorb/query/cleanup/breakdown/rebuild-index/reorganize command set is a concrete instantiation of Karpathy's ingest-query-lint triad, with more granularity. Direct reference for T24 synthesis adoption skill design.

Extraction action: Map these 7 commands to T24 synthesis adoption skill commands. Which map directly, which need adaptation, which are missing?

site.title: Personal Knowledge Wiki Skill
site.description: Claude Code skill for compiling personal data into a knowledge wiki
site.author: farzaa

absence_patterns: No session continuity or compaction handling
No schema versioning or migration path
No evidence of real-world testing at scale
No discussion of context window limits during absorb
No integration with external tools (MCP, search engines)
key_claims: Writer identity produces better knowledge synthesis than filing clerk identity
Anti-cramming and anti-thinning balance produces optimal wiki density
15-entry checkpoint cycle maintains quality during long absorb runs
Parallel 5-agent batches are optimal for cleanup audits
Concept articles (patterns/themes) are the most valuable wiki pages
Diary-driven structure is the most common quality problem
